posts.text:
"I don't think Russia has the capability to [come to Iran's defence]. Russia is currently completely massively, sunk in in Ukraine." Ukraine's former defense minister, Andriy Zagorodnyuk believes Russia "can't actually help Iran apart from shipping some weapons systems and some technologies, maybe even money and definitely intelligence."

Video

"It keeps me up at night that there is no clear end to this conflict as yet," says @elerianm, pointing to "the very difficult task of ending an asymmetrical war." He assesses that the risk of recession is "not overwhelming, but it is high enough for you to worry about."

Video

"We risk going from short-term disruption to long-term structural damage to the oil market," @elerianm tells me. "The increase in the oil price is going to persist for a while and that is going to mean not only higher interest rates, but lower growth, higher unemployment and a greater risk of financial instability."

Video

“The beneficiary of this war [in Iran] is Putin, filling up his coffers with oil dollars… and the reduction in support being given to Ukraine,” says former NATO deputy commander Richard Shirreff. Europe & Canada “must absolutely double down… increasing support for Ukraine.”

Video

“Iran's military capability… has been severely degraded. So in that sense, in a sort of attritional, industrial warfare sense, America and Israel have the upper hand,” says former NATO dep. commander Richard Shirreff. “But right now, because of the Strait of Hormuz… I think President Trump is in danger of looking pretty stupid now, because clearly he and his military planners did not wargame the effect… of their operations.”

Video

“Trust in America [within] the alliance has been broken,” says fmr. NATO dep. commander Richard Shirreff. With allies so far refusing to get involved in the Strait of Hormuz, “I’m not surprised there is this real reluctance to dance to Trump’s tune,” Shirreff adds. “This is a war he started.”

Video

100K YouTube subscribers and counting 🎉

Whether you’ve been here since day one or you’ve recently discovered the podcast - thank you for listening, watching and supporting Christiane Amanpour Presents: The Ex Files 🗂️

Stay tuned for more!

#YouTube #podcast #announcements

Video

Amid a backdrop of war, this Sunday’s Oscars will celebrate some of the greatest art of the past year. Among those in contention: the Iranian filmmakers behind the documentary “Cutting Through Rocks”, who tell me they’re hopeful for change.

Video

From the archives, a look inside the religious fervor that fuels Iran’s military. In 1985, CNN got an exclusive look at the Basij and how children were used as human minesweepers on the country’s frontlines during the Iran-Iraq war.

Video

This week, Iran and Hezbollah launched their first joint attacks on Israel, while Iran has been using cluster munitions to try and pierce Israeli defences. @JDiamond1 saw the destruction on the ground, in this report.

Video
Back in 2015, I spoke with John about his incredible reporting, from Bosnia to Afghanistan to Iraq. You can watch that conversation here: edition.cnn.com/videos/world…

A true loss to journalism and a brilliant career. He was one of the frontline journo-warriors in Sarajevo. RIP.


The New York Times (@nytimes)

Breaking News: John F. Burns, a Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist for The New York Times best known for his work in Iraq, died at 81. nyti.ms/4lp2qq6




— https://nitter.net/nytimes/status/2032484958208422066#m
